epics.title: Workflow Discipline & Issue Lifecycle Improvements
epics.description: Improvements to enforce proper issue workflow and manage issue/epic lifecycle. Includes soft-blocking file writes without an active issue, and a deferred status to park items without closing them.
epics.body:
## Overview
Two complementary improvements to make the issue lifecycle more robust:

1. **Soft-block file writes** — enforce that the agent only modifies files when an issue is actively in-progress, via layered policy injection and tool-result warnings.
2. **Deferred status** — allow issues and epics to be parked without closing them, keeping the active view clean while preserving context.

## Implementation Plan

### Soft-block file writes (thlp7pym)
- `before_agent_start`: inject full workflow policy into system prompt explaining the issue lifecycle and the rule that file writes require an in-progress issue.
- `turn_start`: inject a lightweight status line each turn (`📍 Current: [issue:id] Title (in-progress)` or `⚠️ No issue in-progress`).
- `tool_result` hook on `write`, `edit`, and `bash` (matching write patterns: `>`, `>>`, `tee`, `sed -i`, `mv`, `cp`): append a soft warning when no issue is in-progress.
- **File:** `index.ts`

### Deferred status (fqdkjlyp)
- Add `deferred` as a valid status for issues and epics (side-channel, not part of the sequential flow).
- New tools: `issue_defer` / `epic_defer` (with optional reason), `issue_resume` / `epic_resume` (restores previous status).
- `issue_list` / `epic_list`: exclude deferred by default, add `include_deferred` flag.
- `next_work`: ignore deferred items entirely.
- **Files:** `types.ts`, `issues.ts`, `epics.ts`, `store.ts`, `tool-docs.ts`
successCriteria: Before-agent-start hook injects a clear workflow policy explaining that file writes require an in-progress issue
Each turn start injects a single-line status showing the current in-progress issue or a warning that none is active
Write/edit/bash tool results append a visible soft warning when no issue is in-progress and a file mutation pattern is detected

A closeReason field (done/deferred/wont-fix) exists on issues and epics, set via existing issue_close/epic_close tools — replaces the originally planned issue_defer/epic_defer tools (rejected: unnecessary complexity)
Deferring an epic auto-defers all linked open issues; reopening restores them — replaces the originally planned issue_resume/epic_resume tools (rejected: issue_reopen/epic_reopen already handle this)
issue_list and epic_list exclude deferred items by default, and include them when include_deferred: true is passed
All changes are documented in tool-docs.ts and behave consistently with existing tools

research.content: The soft-block approach is intentionally a **warning**, not a hard block — the agent can still proceed but is reminded of the policy. This avoids workflow friction while still enforcing discipline through visibility. The `tool_result` hook is the most effective layer since it fires at the moment of the violation.

epics.closeMessage: All workflow discipline improvements delivered. Soft-block write gate, per-turn status injection, and closeReason (deferred/wont-fix) implemented. Original defer/resume tools rejected in favour of simpler closeReason on existing close tools.

validations.criterion: Before-agent-start hook injects a clear workflow policy explaining that file writes require an in-progress issue
validations.evidence: before_agent_start in index.ts always injects [WORKFLOW POLICY] line — confirmed live in every turn context

validations.criterion: Each turn start injects a single-line status showing the current in-progress issue or a warning that none is active
validations.evidence: before_agent_start injects '📍 Current: [id] title (in-progress)' or '⚠️ No issue in-progress' — confirmed live in testing

validations.criterion: Write/edit/bash tool results append a visible soft warning when no issue is in-progress and a file mutation pattern is detected
validations.evidence: tool_result gate in index.ts fires on write/edit and bash write patterns (>, >>, tee, sed -i, mv, cp etc.) — tested live: Write tool and echo > triggered gate, ls/cat did not

validations.criterion: A closeReason field (done/deferred/wont-fix) exists on issues and epics, set via existing issue_close/epic_close tools — replaces the originally planned issue_defer/epic_defer tools (rejected: unnecessary complexity)
validations.evidence: closeReason: 'done'|'deferred'|'wont-fix' added to Issue and Epic types; issue_close/epic_close accept close_reason param — tested live

validations.criterion: Deferring an epic auto-defers all linked open issues; reopening restores them — replaces the originally planned issue_resume/epic_resume tools (rejected: issue_reopen/epic_reopen already handle this)
validations.evidence: epic_close with deferred cascades to all linked open issues; epic_reopen restores auto-deferred issues — tested live with cascade count in output

validations.criterion: issue_list and epic_list exclude deferred items by default, and include them when include_deferred: true is passed
validations.evidence: include_deferred flag added to issue_list and epic_list — tested: deferred items hidden by default, visible with include_deferred:true

validations.criterion: next_work never surfaces deferred items
validations.evidence: Deferred items are closed (status=closed), already excluded from next_work which ignores closed items — no changes needed

validations.criterion: All changes are documented in tool-docs.ts and behave consistently with existing tools
validations.evidence: tool-docs.ts updated for issue_close, epic_close, issue_list, epic_list, issue_reopen, epic_reopen with closeReason docs and human-approval notes

epics.title: Config & Git Workflow Integration
epics.description: Adds a typed config system to the project manager and builds git workflow guards on top of it. Config enables runtime toggling of behaviour without code changes; git integration enforces branch discipline and commit hygiene throughout the issue/epic lifecycle.

todos.text: Implement config system (registry, defaults, /config TUI widget)
Implement git workflow integration (guards, branch management, commit tracking)

epics.closeMessage: All config and git workflow integration work is complete. Config system with /config TUI widget shipped; git guards enforcing branch discipline and commit hygiene are live.

epics.title: Issue Traceability & File Capture
epics.description: Adds automatic file-change tracking to in-progress issues, giving each issue a living record of what was touched. Optionally gates closure behind per-file change notes for full traceability.
epics.body:
## Goal
Automatically track which files are created/written/edited during an in-progress issue and surface them as references on that issue. Optionally require a one-sentence change note per file before the issue can be closed.

## Approach
1. Two new config flags (`issues.capture_edited_files`, `issues.require_file_change_notes`), both default `false`
2. Hook pi write/edit tool events in `index.ts` — when capture is enabled and an issue is in-progress, add a `reference` research note with a distinguishable `[auto]` prefix and the file path (deduplicated)
3. Close gate in issue close logic — when `require_file_change_notes` is enabled, scan auto-captured references and block if any lack a follow-up change note

## Files Affected
- `config.ts` — new config entries
- `index.ts` — tool event hooks for write/edit
- `issues.ts` — close gate logic
- `commands.ts` — potentially surface auto-refs in issue display

relevantFiles.reason: Add two new bool config entries under issues prefix
Hook write/edit tool events to capture file references
May need updates to display auto-captured references

todos.text: Implement auto-capture of edited files as issue references (config-gated)

successCriteria: Config flags exist, default to false, and appear in /config widget under 'issues' group
When capture_edited_files is on and an issue is in-progress, every file passed to write/edit is added as a reference note (deduplicated)
When require_file_change_notes is on, issue close is blocked with a clear error listing files missing change notes
When both flags are off (default), behaviour is identical to current — no references captured, no close gate

research.content: Auto-captured file references use a `[auto]` prefix in the research note content so the close gate can distinguish them from manually added references. Change notes are added by the agent as a follow-up research note referencing the same file path.

epics.closeMessage: All file traceability features implemented and user-confirmed. Auto-capture and close gate both working correctly, gated behind config flags that default off.

validations.criterion: Config flags exist, default to false, and appear in /config widget under 'issues' group
validations.evidence: Config flags issues.capture_edited_files and issues.require_file_change_notes added, both default false, visible in /config widget under 'issues' group.

validations.criterion: When capture_edited_files is on and an issue is in-progress, every file passed to write/edit is added as a reference note (deduplicated)
validations.evidence: Confirmed via live test: write tool auto-adds [auto] file reference to in-progress issue, deduplicated on repeat writes.

validations.criterion: When require_file_change_notes is on, issue close is blocked with a clear error listing files missing change notes
validations.evidence: Confirmed via live test: issue_close blocked with clear error listing files missing [change-note] comments.

validations.criterion: When both flags are off (default), behaviour is identical to current — no references captured, no close gate
validations.evidence: Both flags default false; behaviour identical to pre-feature when both are off.

issues.title: Live-updating web dashboard with `/project-serve` command
issues.description:
Add a `/project-serve` command that starts a local web server serving a read-only, live-updating dashboard of the project's epics, issues, and assets. Uses [json-render](https://github.com/vercel-labs/json-render) with shadcn components for rendering the project database. Every element has a "copy reference" button so users can paste precise references back into the agent chat.

**Architecture:**
- **Server (extension-side):** Node.js `http` module — serves static HTML + SSE endpoint. Watches `database.json` for changes and pushes updates.
- **Client (browser):** Single HTML page loading React + `@json-render/shadcn` from CDN (esm.sh). Renders a spec generated from the database. SSE connection for live updates.
- **No npm dependencies** — everything client-side loads via CDN, server uses only Node.js built-ins.

**Multi-project support:**
- Auto-finds an available port (starting from 3100, incrementing)
- URL includes a project identifier derived from cwd, e.g. `http://localhost:3142/project/a1b2c3/`

**Copy-reference:**
- Each element (epic, issue, asset, todo, research note) has a hover "📋" button
- Copies a reference like `[epic:abc123] Title` for pasting into agent chat

**Files affected:**
- New `dashboard-server.ts` — HTTP server, file watcher, SSE, spec generation
- `commands.ts` — register `/project-serve` command
- `index.ts` — wire up the new command

research.content:
## json-render Research

### What it is
json-render is a "Generative UI" framework from Vercel Labs. You define a **catalog** of components with typed props (via Zod), then render a JSON **spec** (tree of `{type, props, children}` elements) through a **registry** of component implementations.

### Key package: `@json-render/shadcn`
- 36 pre-built shadcn/ui components: Card, Stack, Grid, Heading, Text, Badge, Table, Accordion, Alert, Progress, Separator, Button, Input, Select, etc.
- Perfect for a read-only dashboard — Table, Card, Badge, Stack, Heading, Text, Alert, Accordion, Progress cover all our needs.

### Architecture Challenge
json-render is a **React framework** — it needs React, ReactDOM, and a build step (or CDN). The pi extension runs as interpreted TypeScript inside the agent. We need to bridge these:

**Proposed approach:**
1. **Server side (extension):** Use Node.js `http` module to start a server. Serve static HTML + SSE endpoint for live updates. Watch `database.json` for changes.
2. **Client side (browser):** A single HTML file that loads React + json-render + shadcn from CDN (esm.sh or unpkg). Renders the project database as a json-render spec. Connects to SSE for live updates.

### Spec Generation
The extension will need a `specFromDatabase()` function that transforms `database.json` into a json-render spec:
- Epics → Card with status badges, todos as checklists, linked issues
- Issues → Table rows or Cards with type icons, status badges
- Assets → Grouped by category in Accordion sections
- Each element gets a `data-ref` attribute for copy-reference functionality

### Multi-project / Unique URLs
- Auto-find available port (try 3100+, increment)
- URL path includes project identifier (hash of cwd or project name)
- e.g. `http://localhost:3142/project/a1b2c3/`

### Dependencies concern
- No npm dependencies needed in the extension itself — everything loads via CDN on the client
- Server is just Node.js `http` + `fs.watch`
- The spec JSON is generated server-side from the database

## Available shadcn Components (relevant subset)

| Component | Use case in dashboard |
|-----------|----------------------|
| **Card** | Epic cards, issue cards, asset cards |
| **Stack** | Layout — vertical/horizontal grouping |
| **Grid** | Multi-column layouts (e.g. epic grid) |
| **Heading** | Section headers (h1-h3) |
| **Text** | Descriptions, body text, muted captions |
| **Badge** | Status labels (draft/ready/in-progress/closed), issue types (bug/feature/chore) |
| **Table** | Issue lists, asset lists |
| **Accordion** | Collapsible sections (research notes, asset categories) |
| **Alert** | Warnings, info banners |
| **Progress** | Epic completion (todos done/total) |
| **Separator** | Visual dividers |
| **Tabs** | Switch between Epics / Issues / Assets views |

### Copy-Reference Design
Each rendered element wraps in a container with:
- A small "📋" button on hover
- Clicking copies a reference like `[epic:abc123] Title` or `[issue:def456] Title`
- User pastes this into agent chat for precise linking

autoValidation.strategy: User confirms the dashboard serves, live-updates, and copy-reference works in browser

issues.closeMessage: Dashboard server fully implemented with SSE live updates, json-render/shadcn UI, multi-project support, and top-level copy-reference buttons. Sub-item copy buttons tracked in ugf7af4l.

validations.criterion:
Add a `/project-serve` command that starts a local web server serving a read-only, live-updating dashboard of the project's epics, issues, and assets. Uses [json-render](https://github.com/vercel-labs/json-render) with shadcn components for rendering the project database. Every element has a "copy reference" button so users can paste precise references back into the agent chat.

**Architecture:**
- **Server (extension-side):** Node.js `http` module — serves static HTML + SSE endpoint. Watches `database.json` for changes and pushes updates.
- **Client (browser):** Single HTML page loading React + `@json-render/shadcn` from CDN (esm.sh). Renders a spec generated from the database. SSE connection for live updates.
- **No npm dependencies** — everything client-side loads via CDN, server uses only Node.js built-ins.

**Multi-project support:**
- Auto-finds an available port (starting from 3100, incrementing)
- URL includes a project identifier derived from cwd, e.g. `http://localhost:3142/project/a1b2c3/`

**Copy-reference:**
- Each element (epic, issue, asset, todo, research note) has a hover "📋" button
- Copies a reference like `[epic:abc123] Title` for pasting into agent chat

**Files affected:**
- New `dashboard-server.ts` — HTTP server, file watcher, SSE, spec generation
- `commands.ts` — register `/project-serve` command
- `index.ts` — wire up the new command
validations.evidence: All files exist and are wired up: dashboard-server.ts (926 lines), dashboard.ts (597 lines), commands.ts has /project-serve and /project-serve-stop registered. User confirmed "It is created". Copy-reference for sub-items tracked separately in ugf7af4l.

issues.title: Add copy-reference buttons to all atomic dashboard elements
issues.description:
Currently the dashboard's 📋 copy-reference button only appears on top-level Cards (epics/issues/assets). Sub-items like questions, research notes, todos, linked issues, success criteria, and asset sources have no copy button, making it impossible to reference them precisely in agent chat.

**Scope:** Extend `RefWrap` usage or add inline copy buttons to all atomic units:
- **Questions** → `[issue:id:q:0] question text`
- **Research notes** → `[issue:id:research:0] title` / `[epic:id:research:0]`
- **Todos** → `[epic:id:todo:0] todo text`
- **Linked issues** → `[issue:linkedId] title`
- **Success criteria** → `[epic:id:sc:0] criterion text`
- **Asset sources** → `[asset:id:source:0] path`

**Files affected:** `dashboard-server.ts` only — modify HTML rendering sections (~lines 213-260, 292-330) to wrap each sub-item with copy-reference functionality.

research.content:
## Current State Audit

**HAS copy buttons (via `RefWrap` on Card component, line 651):**
- ✅ Top-level Cards: epics, issues, assets

**MISSING copy buttons — all sub-items rendered as raw HTML or json-render Text elements without `RefWrap`:**
- ❌ Questions (lines ~213-230, HTML `question-card` divs)
- ❌ Research notes (lines ~234-244, HTML `<details>` elements)
- ❌ Todos (lines ~292-298, json-render Text elements)
- ❌ Linked issues (lines ~200-208, HTML `<a>` elements)
- ❌ Success criteria (lines ~302-305, json-render Text elements)
- ❌ Relevant files (lines ~308-312, json-render Text elements)
- ❌ Asset sources (not checked but likely same pattern)

**Implementation notes:**
- Issues use raw HTML rendering (`isHtml: true`), so copy buttons need inline HTML copy buttons (not `RefWrap` which is React)
- Epics use json-render spec elements, so they could use `RefWrap` — but sub-items are plain `Text` nodes without ref metadata
- The `copyRef()` JS function and `.copy-ref` CSS already exist and can be reused

issues.title: Soft-block file writes when no issue is in-progress
issues.description:
Enforce proper issue workflow with a layered approach:

1. **`before_agent_start`** — injects full workflow policy into system prompt describing the issue lifecycle (draft → researched → ready → in-progress → closed) and the rule that file writes are only allowed when an issue is in-progress.
2. **`turn_start`** — injects a single status line like `📍 Current: [issue:abc] Title (in-progress)` or `⚠️ No issue in-progress` as a lightweight reminder each turn.
3. **`tool_result`** on `write`/`edit`/`bash` (write patterns like `>`, `>>`, `tee`, `sed -i`, `mv`, `cp`) — appends a warning when no issue is in-progress. This is the actual soft gate.

**File:** `index.ts`

autoValidation.strategy: User confirms the warning appears on write/edit when no issue is in-progress, and that the turn_start reminder and initial policy injection work correctly.

issues.closeMessage: Implemented: before_agent_start always injects status + workflow policy; tool_result gate fires on write/edit and bash write patterns only; fixed tool name casing (lowercase).

validations.criterion:
Enforce proper issue workflow with a layered approach:

1. **`before_agent_start`** — injects full workflow policy into system prompt describing the issue lifecycle (draft → researched → ready → in-progress → closed) and the rule that file writes are only allowed when an issue is in-progress.
2. **`turn_start`** — injects a single status line like `📍 Current: [issue:abc] Title (in-progress)` or `⚠️ No issue in-progress` as a lightweight reminder each turn.
3. **`tool_result`** on `write`/`edit`/`bash` (write patterns like `>`, `>>`, `tee`, `sed -i`, `mv`, `cp`) — appends a warning when no issue is in-progress. This is the actual soft gate.

**File:** `index.ts`
validations.evidence: User confirmed: "Its perfect!" — all three gate scenarios validated live.

issues.title: Add "deferred" close reason for issues and epics
issues.description:
Park issues/epics as "deferred" rather than adding a new status. Uses a `closeReason` validated enum field on close. Deferred items are hidden from default listings but identifiable via `include_deferred` flag.

**Rules:**
- Deferring an issue or epic requires **human approval** — an agent cannot defer autonomously
- Deferring an epic **automatically defers all linked open issues**
- Deferred items are closed with `closeReason: "deferred"` (vs default `"done"`)
- `issue_reopen` / `epic_reopen` brings deferred items back

**Implementation:**
- `types.ts` — add `closeReason: "done" | "deferred" | "wont-fix"` field to Issue and Epic
- `issues.ts` / `epics.ts` — validate `closeReason` on close; epic defer cascades to linked open issues
- `issue_list` / `epic_list` — exclude deferred by default (already excluded as closed), add `include_deferred` flag to surface them
- `tool-docs.ts` — update `issue_close` / `epic_close` docs with `closeReason` field and human-approval note
- `next_work` — no changes needed (deferred items are closed, already ignored)

**Human approval enforcement:**
- The `issue_close` and `epic_close` tools must document that `closeReason: "deferred"` requires explicit user confirmation before the agent may call it

research.content: `deferred` is a side-channel status — not part of the sequential draft→researched→ready→in-progress→closed flow. Items retain their original status internally so `resume` can restore them. Implementation touches: `types.ts` (union), `issues.ts` / `epics.ts` (defer/resume logic), `store.ts` (persistence), `tool-docs.ts` (new tool docs), and list/next_work filtering.

autoValidation.strategy: Defer an issue, verify it disappears from issue_list, verify it shows with include_deferred, resume it and verify it returns to previous status.

issues.closeMessage: Implemented closeReason (done/deferred/wont-fix) on issues and epics. Deferred requires human approval, epic defer cascades to linked issues, reopen restores cascade. Fully tested.

validations.criterion:
Park issues/epics as "deferred" rather than adding a new status. Uses a `closeReason` validated enum field on close. Deferred items are hidden from default listings but identifiable via `include_deferred` flag.

**Rules:**
- Deferring an issue or epic requires **human approval** — an agent cannot defer autonomously
- Deferring an epic **automatically defers all linked open issues**
- Deferred items are closed with `closeReason: "deferred"` (vs default `"done"`)
- `issue_reopen` / `epic_reopen` brings deferred items back

**Implementation:**
- `types.ts` — add `closeReason: "done" | "deferred" | "wont-fix"` field to Issue and Epic
- `issues.ts` / `epics.ts` — validate `closeReason` on close; epic defer cascades to linked open issues
- `issue_list` / `epic_list` — exclude deferred by default (already excluded as closed), add `include_deferred` flag to surface them
- `tool-docs.ts` — update `issue_close` / `epic_close` docs with `closeReason` field and human-approval note
- `next_work` — no changes needed (deferred items are closed, already ignored)

**Human approval enforcement:**
- The `issue_close` and `epic_close` tools must document that `closeReason: "deferred"` requires explicit user confirmation before the agent may call it
validations.evidence: Validated via live tool testing: (1) defer without user approval → blocked with ⛔; (2) defer with approval → issue closed as 📦 deferred; (3) missing from issue_list; (4) visible with include_deferred=true; (5) issue_reopen restored to draft and cleared closeReason; (6) epic defer without user_approval → blocked; (7) epic defer with user_approval → cascaded to linked issues; (8) epic_reopen restored both epic and auto-deferred issues.

issues.title: issue_close silently fails to close when met: false — issue stays in-progress
issues.description:
When calling `issue_close` with `met: false` (e.g. skipping or abandoning an issue), the tool returns an error-style message (`❌ Requirement not met`) but **does not update the issue status to `closed`**. The issue remains stuck in `in-progress`, blocking other issues from starting and polluting `next_work`.

**Root cause:** The early-return guard at step 2 (`if (!params.met) return ...`) fires before `issue.status = "closed"` is ever reached. A `met: false` close is a legitimate workflow action (abandoned, skipped, out-of-scope) and should still transition the issue to `closed`.

**Fix:** When `met: false`, still close the issue (set `status = "closed"`, persist, clean up `closeReviewed`) but record `met: false` in the validation — consistent with how epic_close handles unmet criteria.

**File:** `issues.ts` ~line 313

autoValidation.strategy: Call issue_close with met: false on a test in-progress issue, then confirm its status is closed via issue_show and that next_work no longer surfaces it.

validations.criterion:
When calling `issue_close` with `met: false` (e.g. skipping or abandoning an issue), the tool returns an error-style message (`❌ Requirement not met`) but **does not update the issue status to `closed`**. The issue remains stuck in `in-progress`, blocking other issues from starting and polluting `next_work`.

**Root cause:** The early-return guard at step 2 (`if (!params.met) return ...`) fires before `issue.status = "closed"` is ever reached. A `met: false` close is a legitimate workflow action (abandoned, skipped, out-of-scope) and should still transition the issue to `closed`.

**Fix:** When `met: false`, still close the issue (set `status = "closed"`, persist, clean up `closeReviewed`) but record `met: false` in the validation — consistent with how epic_close handles unmet criteria.

**File:** `issues.ts` ~line 313
validations.evidence: Fixed: removed early-return on met:false, now closes the issue with met:false recorded in validations. Tested: issue_close with met:false correctly sets status=closed, shows ❌ Closed (unmet) label, is no longer in-progress, and does not block next_work.

issues.closeMessage: Fixed met:false early-return in issue_close — now closes the issue and records unmet validation instead of returning an error and leaving it in-progress.

issues.title: Unassigned bucket: future parking lot with smart context injection
issues.description:
Issues without an epic go into a virtual **"Unassigned"** bucket and are treated as **future/parked** — they never appear in `next_work` and are excluded from full session context. The project status header injects only a lightweight summary (e.g. *"3 unassigned ideas, 1 unassigned feature"*) so the agent is aware they exist without polluting context.

**Bugs are the exception** — unassigned bugs are always fully listed at session start since they are immediately relevant regardless of epic assignment.

To make an unassigned non-bug item workable, it must be explicitly assigned to an epic first.

`issue_list` gets a new optional `unassigned: boolean` filter parameter — when `true`, returns only issues with no `epic_id`. This is the explicit way to view and triage the parking lot.

research.content: Files to change: `issues.ts` (add `unassigned` filter to `issue_list`), `next-work.ts` (remove unplanned section from `next_work`), `format.ts` (`formatBrief` and `formatDashboard` - replace full unplanned list with count-by-type summary + full bug listing), `index.ts` (`before_agent_start` - inject unassigned bugs fully, others as count only)

issues.closeMessage: Implemented the unassigned parking-lot bucket across all touch points

validations.criterion:
Issues without an epic go into a virtual **"Unassigned"** bucket and are treated as **future/parked** — they never appear in `next_work` and are excluded from full session context. The project status header injects only a lightweight summary (e.g. *"3 unassigned ideas, 1 unassigned feature"*) so the agent is aware they exist without polluting context.

**Bugs are the exception** — unassigned bugs are always fully listed at session start since they are immediately relevant regardless of epic assignment.

To make an unassigned non-bug item workable, it must be explicitly assigned to an epic first.

`issue_list` gets a new optional `unassigned: boolean` filter parameter — when `true`, returns only issues with no `epic_id`. This is the explicit way to view and triage the parking lot.
validations.evidence: All four touch points implemented and verified via grep: issue_list has unassigned param, next_work gates non-bug unassigned issues, formatBrief/formatDashboard show bugs in full + others as count, before_agent_start injects unassigned bugs fully and other types as a count summary

issues.title: Test unassigned parking lot feature
issues.description: Temporary test issue to verify unassigned bucket behavior — no epic linked.
issues.closeMessage: Test issue — closing after successful unassigned bucket verification

validations.criterion: Temporary test issue to verify unassigned bucket behavior — no epic linked.
validations.evidence: Temporary test issue, no longer needed

issues.title: Test unassigned bug
issues.description: Temporary test bug — no epic linked, should always be visible.
issues.closeMessage: Test issue — closing after successful unassigned bug visibility verification

validations.criterion: Temporary test bug — no epic linked, should always be visible.
validations.evidence: Temporary test issue, no longer needed

issues.title: Config system with registry, defaults, and TUI widget
issues.description:
Adds a typed config registry to the project manager so runtime behaviour can be toggled without editing source. Config lives as its own object in `database.json` with hardcoded defaults that are always applied as fallback — so a "reset to defaults" is trivially possible. Solves the problem of hardcoded behaviour (e.g. workflow gates, context verbosity, future knobs) that currently requires a code change to adjust.

## Implementation

### `config.ts` (new)
Defines a `ConfigRegistry` — a typed map of keys to descriptors:
```ts
{ key, label, description, type: "bool" | "string" | "select", default, options? }
```
Exports `getConfig(r)` (merges DB values over defaults) and `setConfig(r, key, value)`.

### `types.ts`
Add `config: Record<string, unknown>` to `ProjectFile`. Bump `CURRENT_VERSION` for migration.

### `migrations.ts`
Migration to add empty `config: {}` to existing databases.

### `commands.ts`
`/config` command: renders all registry entries with current values in a TUI widget — booleans as toggleable checkboxes, selects as a cycling picker, strings as an inline edit prompt. Selecting an item updates it immediately via `setConfig`.

### `tool-docs.ts`
Document the config keys so the agent knows what's available.

## Initial config keys
| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `workflow.write_gate` | bool | `true` | Enable/disable the file-write gate |
| `context.unassigned_bugs_in_steering` | bool | `true` | Show unassigned bugs in per-turn steering |
| `context.brief_verbosity` | select | `"normal"` | `"minimal"` / `"normal"` / `"verbose"` |

research.content: Files to create/change: new `config.ts` (registry + getConfig/setConfig), `types.ts` (add `config` field to ProjectFile, bump CURRENT_VERSION), `migrations.ts` (add migration for config field), `commands.ts` (add `/config` TUI command), `index.ts` (wire getConfig into write gate and steering), `tool-docs.ts` (document config keys). TUI widget uses pi's List/Input components for bool toggle, select cycling, string editing.

validations.criterion:
Adds a typed config registry to the project manager so runtime behaviour can be toggled without editing source. Config lives as its own object in `database.json` with hardcoded defaults that are always applied as fallback — so a "reset to defaults" is trivially possible. Solves the problem of hardcoded behaviour (e.g. workflow gates, context verbosity, future knobs) that currently requires a code change to adjust.

## Implementation

### `config.ts` (new)
Defines a `ConfigRegistry` — a typed map of keys to descriptors:
```ts
{ key, label, description, type: "bool" | "string" | "select", default, options? }
```
Exports `getConfig(r)` (merges DB values over defaults) and `setConfig(r, key, value)`.

### `types.ts`
Add `config: Record<string, unknown>` to `ProjectFile`. Bump `CURRENT_VERSION` for migration.

### `migrations.ts`
Migration to add empty `config: {}` to existing databases.

### `commands.ts`
`/config` command: renders all registry entries with current values in a TUI widget — booleans as toggleable checkboxes, selects as a cycling picker, strings as an inline edit prompt. Selecting an item updates it immediately via `setConfig`.

### `tool-docs.ts`
Document the config keys so the agent knows what's available.

## Initial config keys
| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `workflow.write_gate` | bool | `true` | Enable/disable the file-write gate |
| `context.unassigned_bugs_in_steering` | bool | `true` | Show unassigned bugs in per-turn steering |
| `context.brief_verbosity` | select | `"normal"` | `"minimal"` / `"normal"` / `"verbose"` |
validations.evidence: Command renamed from `config` to `project-config` in commands.ts — consistent with /project-serve naming convention.

issues.title: Git workflow integration with config-gated guards
issues.description:
Integrates git into the issue/epic lifecycle so the project manager can enforce branch discipline and commit hygiene automatically. Each guard is individually toggleable via the config system (depends on **[7hq83jxg]**). When `git.require_commit_on_close` is enabled, a commit SHA **must** be attached to the close message — `issue_close` is hard-blocked without it, and the SHA is stored on the issue as a permanent audit trail.

## Config keys (all under `git.*`)
| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `git.enabled` | bool | `false` | Master switch — all guards no-op if false |
| `git.epic_branch` | bool | `true` | Create branch when epic goes in-progress |
| `git.require_clean_worktree` | bool | `true` | Block issue start if worktree is dirty |
| `git.require_epic_branch` | bool | `true` | Block issue start if epic branch doesn't exist |
| `git.require_commit_on_close` | bool | `true` | Block issue close if no new commits since issue started |
| `git.require_commit_id_on_close` | bool | `true` | Block `issue_close` unless a valid commit SHA is provided |
| `git.merge_check_on_epic_close` | bool | `true` | Warn if epic branch not merged when closing epic |

## New files
**`git.ts`** — thin shell wrapper: `isClean()`, `currentBranch()`, `branchExists(name)`, `createBranch(name)`, `commitExists(sha)`, `hasCommitsSince(sha)`.

## Changes to existing files

**`types.ts`** — add `gitBranch?: string` to `Epic`, `startCommit?: string` and `closeCommit?: string` to `Issue`.

**`migrations.ts`** — migration for new optional fields.

**`issue_advance`** → in-progress: check clean worktree + epic branch exists (if guards enabled).

**`issue_close`** — add `commit_id?: string` param. When `git.enabled` + `git.require_commit_id_on_close`: hard-block if missing or SHA invalid (`git cat-file -t <sha>`). Store as `closeCommit` on issue. Show in output and `issue_show`.

**`epic_advance`** → in-progress: auto-create branch `epic/{id}-{slug}` (if `git.epic_branch`).

**`epic_close`** — warn if epic branch not merged into default branch (if `git.merge_check_on_epic_close`).

**`tool-docs.ts`** — document git params and config keys.

## Branch naming
`epic/{epic-id}-{slugified-title}` — issues commit on the epic branch directly.

## Dependency
Implement **after** [7hq83jxg] (config system) — all guards route through it.

research.content: Files: new `git.ts` (shell wrappers: isClean, currentBranch, branchExists, createBranch, commitExists, hasCommitsSince). Hook into: `epics.ts` epic_advance→in-progress (createBranch), `issues.ts` issue_advance→in-progress (clean worktree + epic branch check), `issues.ts` issue_close (commit existence + commit_id param). New fields on types: Epic.gitBranch, Issue.startCommit, Issue.closeCommit. Migration v8→v9. All guards route through getConfigValue with git.enabled master switch.

issues.closeMessage: Git workflow integration fully implemented with all config-gated guards

validations.criterion:
Integrates git into the issue/epic lifecycle so the project manager can enforce branch discipline and commit hygiene automatically. Each guard is individually toggleable via the config system (depends on **[7hq83jxg]**). When `git.require_commit_on_close` is enabled, a commit SHA **must** be attached to the close message — `issue_close` is hard-blocked without it, and the SHA is stored on the issue as a permanent audit trail.

## Config keys (all under `git.*`)
| Key | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `git.enabled` | bool | `false` | Master switch — all guards no-op if false |
| `git.epic_branch` | bool | `true` | Create branch when epic goes in-progress |
| `git.require_clean_worktree` | bool | `true` | Block issue start if worktree is dirty |
| `git.require_epic_branch` | bool | `true` | Block issue start if epic branch doesn't exist |
| `git.require_commit_on_close` | bool | `true` | Block issue close if no new commits since issue started |
| `git.require_commit_id_on_close` | bool | `true` | Block `issue_close` unless a valid commit SHA is provided |
| `git.merge_check_on_epic_close` | bool | `true` | Warn if epic branch not merged when closing epic |

## New files
**`git.ts`** — thin shell wrapper: `isClean()`, `currentBranch()`, `branchExists(name)`, `createBranch(name)`, `commitExists(sha)`, `hasCommitsSince(sha)`.

## Changes to existing files

**`types.ts`** — add `gitBranch?: string` to `Epic`, `startCommit?: string` and `closeCommit?: string` to `Issue`.

**`migrations.ts`** — migration for new optional fields.

**`issue_advance`** → in-progress: check clean worktree + epic branch exists (if guards enabled).

**`issue_close`** — add `commit_id?: string` param. When `git.enabled` + `git.require_commit_id_on_close`: hard-block if missing or SHA invalid (`git cat-file -t <sha>`). Store as `closeCommit` on issue. Show in output and `issue_show`.

**`epic_advance`** → in-progress: auto-create branch `epic/{id}-{slug}` (if `git.epic_branch`).

**`epic_close`** — warn if epic branch not merged into default branch (if `git.merge_check_on_epic_close`).

**`tool-docs.ts`** — document git params and config keys.

## Branch naming
`epic/{epic-id}-{slugified-title}` — issues commit on the epic branch directly.

## Dependency
Implement **after** [7hq83jxg] (config system) — all guards route through it.
validations.evidence: git.ts created with full shell wrappers. types.ts has gitBranch on Epic, startCommit/closeCommit on Issue, bumped to v9. migrations.ts has v8→v9. epics.ts auto-creates branch on in-progress and shows merge check on epic_close. issues.ts blocks issue start on dirty worktree / missing epic branch, records startCommit on start, blocks close without commits / commit_id, stores closeCommit. format.ts shows git fields in issue_show.

issues.title: Rename /config command to /project-config
issues.description: Rename the `/config` command to `/project-config` for consistency with other project manager commands like `/project-serve`.
issues.closeMessage: Closing — will reopen original issue instead

validations.criterion: Rename the `/config` command to `/project-config` for consistency with other project manager commands like `/project-serve`.
validations.evidence: User wants to handle this via the original issue instead

issues.title: Split git config into git.epics.* and git.issues.* sections
issues.description: Currently all git guards live under a flat `git.*` namespace, making it impossible to disable epic-level behaviour (branch creation, merge checks) without also disabling issue-level behaviour (clean worktree, commit tracking). This splits the registry into three sections: `git.enabled` (master switch), `git.epics.*` (branch auto-create, merge check on close), and `git.issues.*` (require clean worktree, require epic branch, require commit on close, require commit ID). Each section can be fully disabled independently while the master switch still kills everything at once.
issues.closeMessage: Split git config into git.epics.* and git.issues.* namespaces — each section independently toggleable

validations.criterion: Currently all git guards live under a flat `git.*` namespace, making it impossible to disable epic-level behaviour (branch creation, merge checks) without also disabling issue-level behaviour (clean worktree, commit tracking). This splits the registry into three sections: `git.enabled` (master switch), `git.epics.*` (branch auto-create, merge check on close), and `git.issues.*` (require clean worktree, require epic branch, require commit on close, require commit ID). Each section can be fully disabled independently while the master switch still kills everything at once.
validations.evidence: config.ts registry updated with git.epics.* and git.issues.* keys. epics.ts and issues.ts updated to use new key names. tool-docs.ts updated. .gitignore fixed. Old flat git.* keys removed.

issues.title: Split git config section into epics/issues subgroups in /config widget
issues.description:
The `/config` TUI widget groups all `git.*` keys under a single "git" heading. The user wants visual separation between epic-related git settings and issue-related git settings.

**Change:** Update the render grouping logic in `commands.ts` so that:
- `git.enabled` stays under heading **`git`**
- `git.epics.*` keys get a subheading **`git · epics`**
- `git.issues.*` keys get a subheading **`git · issues`**

This requires using a deeper key prefix (up to 2 segments) for grouping when the second segment is "epics" or "issues".

autoValidation.strategy: User visually confirms the /config widget shows two separate git subgroups

issues.closeMessage: User confirmed the split looks perfect. Grouping logic updated in commands.ts render() to use 2-segment prefix for git subgroups.

validations.criterion:
The `/config` TUI widget groups all `git.*` keys under a single "git" heading. The user wants visual separation between epic-related git settings and issue-related git settings.

**Change:** Update the render grouping logic in `commands.ts` so that:
- `git.enabled` stays under heading **`git`**
- `git.epics.*` keys get a subheading **`git · epics`**
- `git.issues.*` keys get a subheading **`git · issues`**

This requires using a deeper key prefix (up to 2 segments) for grouping when the second segment is "epics" or "issues".
validations.evidence: User confirmed "PERFECT" — git · epics and git · issues subgroups are visible and correct in /config widget.

issues.title: Add distinct background color to /config widget panel
issues.description: The `/config` TUI widget blends into the terminal background, making it hard to distinguish the widget boundary. Add a background color to each rendered line so the widget panel stands out clearly from the rest of the UI.

autoValidation.strategy: User visually confirms the /config widget has a distinct background that separates it from the terminal background

issues.closeMessage: Applied selectedBg background to all /config widget lines via a Proxy on the lines array. User confirmed it works.

validations.criterion: The `/config` TUI widget blends into the terminal background, making it hard to distinguish the widget boundary. Add a background color to each rendered line so the widget panel stands out clearly from the rest of the UI.
validations.evidence: User confirmed "MUCH better it works as expected".

issues.title: Auto-capture edited files as issue references with optional close-time change notes
issues.description:
When working on an in-progress issue, there's no automatic record of which files were actually touched — that context lives only in git history. This feature adds two opt-in config switches: one that silently captures every file written/edited during an issue and records it as a reference on that issue, and a second that gates issue closure behind a one-sentence "what changed" note per captured file. Both are disabled by default.

## Config entries (both default `false`)
- `issues.capture_edited_files` — auto-add written/edited files as issue references when an issue is in-progress
- `issues.require_file_change_notes` — block issue close until each captured file has a one-sentence change note

## Implementation

**`config.ts`**
- Add two new bool `ConfigEntry` items under the `issues` prefix

**`index.ts`**
- Hook into pi's write/edit tool events; when `issues.capture_edited_files` is enabled and an issue is in-progress, call `issue_research` with `type: "reference"` recording the file path (deduplicate — skip if already captured)

**`issues.ts` / close gate**
- When `issues.require_file_change_notes` is enabled, scan auto-captured file references on the issue at close time; if any lack a change note, block closure with a clear error listing the missing files

**Data model**
- No schema changes needed — auto-captured files use the existing research notes structure with a distinguishable prefix (e.g. `[auto] path/to/file`) so the gate logic can identify them

research.content: [change-note] /tmp/pi-capture-test9.txt — temporary test file used to verify auto-capture and deduplication behaviour.

autoValidation.strategy: User confirms: (1) files are auto-captured as references when config is on, (2) issue close is blocked when require_file_change_notes is on and notes are missing, (3) both default to off

issues.closeMessage: Feature fully working. Auto-capture hooks write/edit tool events, deduplicates, persists references. Close gate enforces change notes per captured file. Fixed missing save import bug found via log file debugging.

validations.criterion:
When working on an in-progress issue, there's no automatic record of which files were actually touched — that context lives only in git history. This feature adds two opt-in config switches: one that silently captures every file written/edited during an issue and records it as a reference on that issue, and a second that gates issue closure behind a one-sentence "what changed" note per captured file. Both are disabled by default.

## Config entries (both default `false`)
- `issues.capture_edited_files` — auto-add written/edited files as issue references when an issue is in-progress
- `issues.require_file_change_notes` — block issue close until each captured file has a one-sentence change note

## Implementation

**`config.ts`**
- Add two new bool `ConfigEntry` items under the `issues` prefix

**`index.ts`**
- Hook into pi's write/edit tool events; when `issues.capture_edited_files` is enabled and an issue is in-progress, call `issue_research` with `type: "reference"` recording the file path (deduplicate — skip if already captured)

**`issues.ts` / close gate**
- When `issues.require_file_change_notes` is enabled, scan auto-captured file references on the issue at close time; if any lack a change note, block closure with a clear error listing the missing files

**Data model**
- No schema changes needed — auto-captured files use the existing research notes structure with a distinguishable prefix (e.g. `[auto] path/to/file`) so the gate logic can identify them
validations.evidence: User confirmed. All three behaviours verified: auto-capture works, deduplication works, close gate blocks on missing change notes, both flags default off.

assets.title: Reload before testing
assets.context: Before running any tests on pi extension functionality after code changes
assets.body:
After making code changes to the extension, always ask the user to reload pi before running any functional tests. The extension runs from TypeScript source directly and changes are not picked up until pi is reloaded.

**Rule:** "Please reload pi before I test this." — wait for confirmation before proceeding with tests.
